why: Protocol 775 Login serverbound minecraft:hello must dispatch from the official framed packet id to Stevenarella's Login serverbound decoder through packet::packet_by_id and consume the official name plus profileId body bytes. This is packet framing/dispatch/decode evidence only and is separate from authentication success, encryption/key exchange, login acknowledgement, Configuration entry, or client-load completion.
what: The Rust oracle test login_hello_serverbound_framed_dispatch_matches_official_oracle_answer panics while exercising packet::packet_by_id(775, State::Login, Direction::Serverbound, packet id 0, official hello body bytes). Protocol 775 currently has no Login state mapping in v26_1_2.rs, so the official Login serverbound table row is unsupported by the active version map.

evidence.summary: Generated from official client.jar direct calls: ServerboundHelloPacket(String, UUID), ServerboundHelloPacket.STREAM_CODEC, LoginProtocols.SERVERBOUND.codec().encode/decode(ServerboundHelloPacket), LoginProtocols.SERVERBOUND_TEMPLATE.details().listPackets(...), and decoded field accessors.
Manifest declares the exact Rust oracle test and framed_dispatch_decode comparison surface.
Rust test reads the manifest and answer, splits official framed VarInt packet id from body bytes, and calls packet::packet_by_id for Login serverbound hello dispatch.
Current Protocol 775 version table contains Handshaking and Configuration mappings only and has no Login serverbound mapping for official packet id 0x00.

observed_failure: thread 'login_hello_oracle' panicked at stevenarella/protocol/src/protocol/versions/v26_1_2.rs:1:1: bad packet state Login; Stevenarella panicked while dispatching official Login serverbound hello packet id 0
